campaign.title: Customize Your Account Manager
campaign.subject: customize your settings to sound like your business
campaign.description: Train your account manager on your business voice, services, and preferences

landing_page.ai_goal: Guide user through customization of their account manager settings

slides.title: customization

content.headline: Make modern tools Sound Like You
content.subhead: Train your search tools in 20 minutes

slides.narration: Generic modern tools sounds like... generic modern tools. But trained modern tools sounds like your business—your voice, your expertise, your way of helping customers. Let's train yours to be unmistakably you.

slides.title: What You'll Train
slides.narration: We'll train your modern tools on business knowledge—what you do and how you do it. Voice and tone—how you talk to customers. Common scenarios—the situations that come up repeatedly. And boundaries—when modern tools should escalate to a human.

slides.title: Step 1: Business Knowledge
slides.narration: Start with business knowledge. Upload service descriptions and pricing. Add your FAQs—you've already done that work. Include policies on returns, cancellations, guarantees. Hours, location, contact info. And what makes you different from competitors. This becomes the system's knowledge base.

slides.title: Step 2: Voice and Tone
slides.narration: Define your voice and tone. Are you formal or casual? Do you use emojis? Technical jargon or plain language? Warm and personal or efficient and direct? The best way: provide example responses you've actually written. Real emails, real messages. the system learns from your examples.

slides.title: Step 3: Common Scenarios

content.format: For each: Example question → Ideal response

slides.narration: Train on common scenarios. Pricing questions. Scheduling requests. Service inquiries. Complaints. Special requests. For each scenario, provide an example question and your ideal response. The more examples, the better modern tools handles variations.

slides.title: Step 4: Set Boundaries

boundaries: What your account team can and can't promise

slides.narration: Set clear boundaries. Topics modern tools shouldn't discuss—maybe specific pricing negotiations or competitor comparisons. When to escalate to a human—set clear triggers. What your account team can promise versus what needs human approval. How to handle sensitive situations. Escalate when customer asks for manager, mentions legal issues, expresses strong frustration, or has complex custom requests.

slides.title: Pro Tips

tips: Review modern tools conversations weekly at first
Update training when you notice gaps
Test by asking tricky questions yourself

slides.narration: customization pro tips. Review modern tools conversations weekly at first—you'll spot gaps quickly. Update training when you notice modern tools missing something. Test by asking tricky questions yourself before customers do. Use the feedback loop—mark responses as good or bad so modern tools keeps learning.

slides.title: Train Your modern tools
slides.narration: Ready to make modern tools sound like you? Start training now—twenty minutes of setup saves hours of generic responses. Or see training examples from businesses like yours first. Your modern tools should be unmistakably yours.

email_tutorial.subject: Customize how your account manager handles things
email_tutorial.preview_text: Make the system sound like you
email_tutorial.body:
Hi [First Name],

Your account manager handles customer inquiries, content, and follow-ups. Here's how to customize it:

→ Your services and pricing (so responses are accurate)
→ Your voice and tone (so everything sounds like you)
→ Common scenarios (so the right responses go out)
→ When to escalate (so you get the calls that matter)

[CUSTOMIZE YOUR SETTINGS →]

The more you train it, the better it works.

[AM Name]
